mentions.raw_name: Garin (orphan of the Greenblood, Arianne's milk brother)
Daeron I Targaryen / the Young Dragon (historical, referenced)
Aerys II Targaryen / Mad King Aerys (referenced)
Dake (referenced — too fat to be decoy)
Garin's mother (referenced — Arianne's wet nurse, midwife)
Garin's cousin (referenced — broke into Quentyn's strongbox)
Tyene's mother (referenced — lives near the Mander)
Iron Throne (as seat of power/physical object)
The Rhoyne (referenced historically as Mother Rhoyne)
House Oakheart (three green oak leaves sigil)
House Lannister (of Lannisport — Rosamund, Lannys, Lannetts, Lantells, lesser Lannisters)
House Baratheon (referenced via Tommen, Myrcella, Stannis)
The Kingsguard (Ser Arys is a member; referenced as "the finest knights in all the Seven Kingdoms")
The Golden Company ("Beneath the gold the bitter steel"; broke its contract with Myr)
Orphans of the Greenblood (river-dwelling Rhoynar remnant)
The Sand Snakes (Arianne wants to free them)
Faith of the Seven (referenced: the Maiden statue at Shandystone; "this dry red land or its seven-faced god")
Rhoynar river worship (Mother Rhoyne as deity; Old Man of the River; Crab King)
R'hllor / red priests (referenced: followers rioted in Qohor, tried to burn down the Black Goat)
The Black Goat of Qohor (referenced: red priests tried to burn it down)
Rhoynar (historical and the orphans as remnant)
Norvoshi (Lady Mellario's origin; do not foster out their children)
Myrmen (about to go to war with Lys)
Valyrians (referenced historically — "made Valyria tremble"; "children as beautiful as dragonlords")
Arianne's throwing knife (gift from Lady Nym)
Arianne's silk veil (green and yellow, with green pearls)
Quentyn's sealed scrolls (sun and spear of Dorne seal)
Cyvasse set (referenced — Myrcella and Trystane play)
Poleboat (carved and painted in greens, mermaid tiller, fish faces, iron lanterns)
Conquest of Dorne (by Daeron I / the Young Dragon)
